title: Race-lap relative context features instead of global race heuristics
summary: Pit calls are relative decisions against the field, not just absolute tyre age.
rationale: Pit calls are relative decisions against the field, not just absolute tyre age. Game-theoretic and race-strategy sources emphasize competition, traffic, and response behavior. A simpler way to capture this than full ranking models is to encode where a driver sits relative to the field at the same race-lap.
implementation_hint: For each `Year/Race/LapNumber`, compute OOF field-relative percentile and z-score features for `TyreLife`, `LapTime_Delta`, `Cumulative_Degradation`, `Position`, and `Position_Change`, plus nearest-neighbor gaps to cars ahead/behind in tyre age and degradation. Add counts like 'how many peers on same compound/stint exceed this tyre age' and 'is this driver the most over-aged among same-compound runners'.
expected_effect: Should sharpen undercut/cover-stop signals and create a more portable competitive-context layer than hand-picked track priors.
risk: These cross-sectional features are leakage-prone if computed with validation rows mixed into training statistics; build them fold-wise and avoid any use of future laps.
